questions.question: Why can diffusion training pick any timestep t directly instead of simulating the forward Markov chain step by step?
questions.question_zh: 为什么 diffusion 训练可以直接采样任意时间步 t，而不必一步步模拟前向 Markov 链？
options: It is an approximation that ignores the intermediate steps
Because the composition of many Gaussian additions still yields a Gaussian, giving a closed-form q(x_t|x_0) = N(sqrt(alpha_bar_t) x_0, (1 - alpha_bar_t) I) that you can sample in one step
Because x_t is independent of x_0
It is required by PyTorch's autograd
options_zh: 这是一种忽略中间步骤的近似
多次叠加高斯噪声仍然是高斯，能得到闭式 q(x_t|x_0) = N(sqrt(alpha_bar_t) x_0, (1 - alpha_bar_t) I)，一次采样就能得到 x_t
因为 x_t 与 x_0 独立
PyTorch 的 autograd 要求
questions.explanation: Repeated Gaussian additions close up analytically. The cumulative product alpha_bar_t encodes 'how much signal is left after t steps', and you can sample x_t directly without running the chain. This is why diffusion training is O(1) per step rather than O(T).
questions.explanation_zh: 反复叠加高斯噪声在解析上是闭合的。累积乘积 alpha_bar_t 编码了「t 步之后还剩多少信号」，于是可以直接采样 x_t 而不必跑整条链。这也是为什么 diffusion 训练每步是 O(1) 而不是 O(T)。

questions.question: What does a DDPM's neural network actually predict?
questions.question_zh: DDPM 中的神经网络真正预测的是什么？
options: The next image x_{t-1} directly
The noise epsilon that was added at step t, from which x_{t-1}'s mean is derived analytically
The class label of the input
The signal-to-noise ratio
options_zh: 直接预测下一张图 x_{t-1}
预测第 t 步加进来的噪声 epsilon，再用解析公式推导出 x_{t-1} 的均值
输入图像的类别标签
信噪比
questions.explanation: DDPM parameterises the model to predict epsilon. Given the noise prediction and the noise schedule, x_{t-1}'s Gaussian mean and variance are closed-form. Predicting epsilon instead of x_0 or x_{t-1} simplifies the loss to a plain MSE and gives numerically better gradients.
questions.explanation_zh: DDPM 把模型参数化为预测 epsilon。有了噪声预测和噪声调度，x_{t-1} 的高斯均值和方差就是闭式的。预测 epsilon 比预测 x_0 或 x_{t-1} 让 loss 变成简单的 MSE，数值上的梯度也更好。

questions.question: Why does DDIM achieve similar sample quality to DDPM with ~20x fewer steps?
questions.question_zh: 为什么 DDIM 能用大约少 20 倍的步数达到与 DDPM 相近的样本质量？
options: DDIM changes the training objective
DDIM reformulates sampling as a deterministic ODE whose trajectory can be discretised with far fewer steps while hitting nearly the same endpoint; no retraining required
DDIM uses a faster model architecture
DDIM generates smaller images
options_zh: DDIM 改了训练目标
DDIM 把采样重新表述成确定性 ODE，其轨迹可以用很少的步数离散化、却到达几乎相同的终点，不需要重新训练
DDIM 用了更快的模型架构
DDIM 生成的图像更小
questions.explanation: DDIM's key observation is that the reverse process, when re-parameterised deterministically, becomes an ODE that a few steps approximate well. The model (weights and loss) is unchanged from DDPM; only the sampler differs. A DDPM checkpoint can be used with a DDIM, DPM-Solver, or Euler sampler interchangeably.
questions.explanation_zh: DDIM 的关键洞见是：把反向过程换一种确定性参数化后，它变成一个 ODE，用很少的步数就能近似得很好。模型本身（权重和 loss）和 DDPM 一样，区别只在采样器。一个 DDPM 的 checkpoint 可以无缝换上 DDIM、DPM-Solver 或 Euler 采样器。

questions.question: You plot `alpha_bar_t` for a linear beta schedule with T=1000 and see it drops to near-zero by t=600. Why does this matter?
questions.question_zh: 你画出 T=1000 线性 beta 调度下的 `alpha_bar_t` 曲线，发现它到 t=600 就接近 0 了，为什么这是个问题？
options: It does not matter

The last 40% of timesteps add almost no signal; training those timesteps teaches the network to denoise noise that is already approximately N(0, I), which wastes capacity. Cosine or sigmoid schedules fix this by spreading the noise injection more evenly
alpha_bar_t must be zero at the end
PyTorch requires a linear schedule
options_zh: 这不重要
最后 40% 的时间步几乎不再添加信号；这些步训练时只是在让网络去噪一个已近似 N(0, I) 的输入，纯属浪费容量。cosine 或 sigmoid 调度通过更均匀地注入噪声解决了这个问题
alpha_bar_t 末尾必须为 0
PyTorch 要求使用线性调度
questions.explanation: Linear beta schedules finish the diffusion process too early. By t=600 the signal is already destroyed; training and sampling through t=600-1000 adds nothing. Cosine schedules (Nichol & Dhariwal) keep signal around longer, which is why modern models use them, especially at lower resolutions.
questions.explanation_zh: 线性 beta 调度把扩散过程「结束」得太早。到 t=600 信号就已经被毁了，t=600 到 1000 这些步训练和采样都没意义。cosine 调度（Nichol 与 Dhariwal）能让信号保留得更久，所以现代模型——尤其是低分辨率模型——都用 cosine。

questions.question: Why does time conditioning get added to the U-Net via a sinusoidal embedding instead of a one-hot timestep?
questions.question_zh: 为什么 U-Net 的时间条件用正弦 embedding 而不是 one-hot 时间步？
options: One-hot vectors are too large

A sinusoidal embedding is smooth in t, which lets the network interpolate between timesteps it has not seen exactly and encodes scale information the architecture can use additively at multiple depths
Sinusoidal is required by BatchNorm
One-hot would violate the chain rule
options_zh: one-hot 向量太大了
正弦 embedding 关于 t 是平滑的，让网络可以在没精确见过的时间步之间插值，并以多频率的尺度信息让架构在不同深度上加性地使用
BatchNorm 要求使用正弦 embedding
one-hot 会违反链式法则
questions.explanation: Sinusoidal embeddings give the network a continuous representation of t at multiple frequencies. Any timestep is a unique point in that embedding space, and nearby timesteps are nearby embeddings. That lets the model generalise across all T values with far fewer parameters than a learned per-timestep embedding. Same idea as transformer positional encoding.
questions.explanation_zh: 正弦 embedding 在多个频率上给出 t 的连续表示。任意时间步在 embedding 空间里都是唯一的点，相邻时间步的 embedding 也相近。这样模型用比「为每个时间步学一个 embedding」少得多的参数就能泛化到所有 T。和 transformer 的位置编码思路一致。
